title: Advanced count time-series uncertainty and forecast evaluation
text: vignette demonstrates reproducible count time-series workflow explicit uncertainty handling --sample evaluation. emphasis methodology: uncertainty estimation modeling stage, uncertainty propagation predictive distributions, interpretation predictive intervals, rolling-origin backtesting, forecast scoring (MAE, RMSE, interval coverage, WIS, CRPS). also includes chain-based simulation comparison using epichains.

title: Advanced count time-series uncertainty and forecast evaluation
text: prep_incidence_data() enforces regular daily index non-negative counts, essential coherent rolling-origin resampling renewal calculations.

title: Advanced count time-series uncertainty and forecast evaluation
text: fit Poisson log-linear trend model: logE[]=β0+β1t \log E[I_t] = \beta_0 + \beta_1 t uncertainty β1\beta_1 approximated model covariance matrix. β1\beta_1, derive doubling time implied reproduction scale via exp(β1×s‾)\exp(\beta_1 \times \bar{s}), s‾\bar{s} mean serial interval. draw object growth$uncertainty_draws reusable downstream forecasting sensitivity analyses.
code: growth <- fit_poisson_growth(   inc,   n_draws = 1000,   mean_si = 4.7,   seed = 42 )  growth$estimates #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   term  growth_rate std_error conf_low conf_high doubling_time implied_rt #>   <chr>       <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>         <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 day         0.109    0.0196   0.0709     0.148          6.34       1.67 growth_uncertainty <- growth$uncertainty_draws |>   summarise(     rt_median = median(implied_rt),     rt_q10 = quantile(implied_rt, 0.1),     rt_q90 = quantile(implied_rt, 0.9),     growth_q10 = quantile(growth_rate, 0.1),     growth_q90 = quantile(growth_rate, 0.9)   )  growth_uncertainty #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   rt_median rt_q10 rt_q90 growth_q10 growth_q90 #>       <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>      <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1      1.67   1.48   1.88     0.0841      0.134

what: Stage 2: renewal forecast with uncertainty propagation
title: Advanced count time-series uncertainty and forecast evaluation
text: Given serial interval weights wsw_s, renewal forecasts use E[]=Rt∑s=1t−1It−sws E[I_t] = R_t \sum_{s=1}^{t-1} I_{t-s} w_s sample future counts Poisson process. propagate uncertainty using draw-specific R_t trajectories.
code: serial_interval <- c(0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.25, 0.15) h <- 7  # Map growth-rate uncertainty into Rt paths (constant within horizon per draw) rt_matrix <- matrix(   growth$uncertainty_draws$implied_rt,   nrow = nrow(growth$uncertainty_draws),   ncol = h )  forecast_draws <- simulate_renewal_forecast(   incidence = inc$incidence,   serial_interval = serial_interval,   rt = rt_matrix,   horizon = h,   seed = 100 )  forecast_summary <- summarise_forecast_uncertainty(   forecast_draws,   probs = c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9),   interval_level = 0.9 )  forecast_summary #> # A tibble: 7 × 10 #>     day  mean median    sd lower upper interval_level q10.0000 q50.0000 q90.0000 #>   <int> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>          <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1     1  11.8     12  3.58  6       18            0.9        7       12       16 #> 2     2  13.2     13  3.87  7       20            0.9        8       13       18 #> 3     3  16.6     16  4.79  9.35    25            0.9       11       16       23 #> 4     4  19.7     19  5.95 11       30            0.9       12       19       27 #> 5     5  23.5     23  7.92 12       38            0.9       14       23       34 #> 6     6  27.4     26  9.69 14       45            0.9       16       26       41 #> 7     7  32.9     31 12.4  16       57            0.9       19       31       49 last_date <- max(inc$date) plot_df <- forecast_summary |>   mutate(date = last_date + day)  ggplot(plot_df, aes(date, median)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), fill = "#9ecae1", alpha = 0.45) +   geom_line(color = "#08519c", linewidth = 0.9) +   labs(     title = "7-day predictive distribution from renewal simulation",     subtitle = "Median and 90% interval with parameter + process uncertainty",     x = NULL,     y = "Forecast incidence"   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

previous_headings: Stage 2: renewal forecast with uncertainty propagation
title: Advanced count time-series uncertainty and forecast evaluation
text: practical interpretation pattern: Median: central forecast trajectory. 90% interval: expected range model assumptions. Interval width growth horizon: uncertainty accumulation recursive simulation. isolate uncertainty sources, compare fixed-R_t run uncertain R_t draws. decomposition, wider intervals stochastic R_t indicate meaningful parameter-driven uncertainty beyond intrinsic count noise.
code: fixed_rt_draws <- simulate_renewal_forecast(   incidence = inc$incidence,   serial_interval = serial_interval,   rt = median(growth$uncertainty_draws$implied_rt),   horizon = h,   n_sims = 1000,   seed = 101 )  width_compare <- bind_rows(   summarise_forecast_uncertainty(fixed_rt_draws, interval_level = 0.9) |>     transmute(day, width = upper - lower, source = "process only (fixed Rt)"),   summarise_forecast_uncertainty(forecast_draws, interval_level = 0.9) |>     transmute(day, width = upper - lower, source = "process + Rt uncertainty") )  width_compare #> # A tibble: 14 × 3 #>      day width source                   #>    <int> <dbl> <chr>                    #>  1     1  11   process only (fixed Rt)  #>  2     2  12   process only (fixed Rt)  #>  3     3  14   process only (fixed Rt)  #>  4     4  16   process only (fixed Rt)  #>  5     5  21   process only (fixed Rt)  #>  6     6  23   process only (fixed Rt)  #>  7     7  28   process only (fixed Rt)  #>  8     1  12   process + Rt uncertainty #>  9     2  13   process + Rt uncertainty #> 10     3  15.7 process + Rt uncertainty #> 11     4  19   process + Rt uncertainty #> 12     5  26   process + Rt uncertainty #> 13     6  31   process + Rt uncertainty #> 14     7  41   process + Rt uncertainty

title: Advanced count time-series uncertainty and forecast evaluation
text: Rolling-origin evaluation repeatedly trains prefixes time series scores forecasts unseen future observations. backtest$overall provides global summaries across origins horizons: MAE: mean absolute point error, RMSE: root mean squared point error, coverage: empirical 90% interval coverage, WIS: weighted interval score (sharpness + calibration penalties), CRPS: proper score full predictive distributions.
code: backtest <- rolling_origin_evaluate(   data = inc,   initial_window = 14,   horizon = 5,   step = 1,   serial_interval = serial_interval,   rt_method = "growth",   n_sims = 400,   interval_level = 0.9,   wis_levels = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95),   seed = 2026 )  backtest$overall #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>     mae  rmse coverage   wis  crps n_origins n_forecasts #>   <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <int>       <int> #> 1  1.78  2.33        1  1.02  1.34         5          25 backtest$origin_summary #> # A tibble: 5 × 7 #>   origin_index origin_date   mae  rmse coverage   wis  crps #>          <dbl> <date>      <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1           14 2024-01-14    0.8  1.41        1 0.710 0.916 #> 2           15 2024-01-15    1.7  2.54        1 0.932 1.23  #> 3           16 2024-01-16    1.8  2.32        1 1.05  1.35  #> 4           17 2024-01-17    2    2.37        1 1.09  1.47  #> 5           18 2024-01-18    2.6  2.79        1 1.31  1.74 ggplot(backtest$scores, aes(target_date, wis, group = origin_index, color = as.factor(origin_index))) +   geom_line(alpha = 0.7) +   labs(     title = "Rolling-origin WIS by target date",     x = NULL,     y = "WIS",     color = "Origin"   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

title: Advanced count time-series uncertainty and forecast evaluation
text: omia includes simulate_epichains_forecast() branching-process alternative. compare renewal vs epichains forecasts training origin scalar R_t estimate. side--side comparison useful teaching different stochastic assumptions (renewal-Poisson vs explicit transmission chains) change predictive sharpness calibration.
code: # One explicit holdout split for method comparison train_n <- nrow(inc) - 7 train <- inc[1:train_n, ] truth <- inc$incidence[(train_n + 1):nrow(inc)]  fit_train <- fit_poisson_growth(train, n_draws = 500, seed = 44) rt_hat <- median(fit_train$uncertainty_draws$implied_rt)  renewal_cmp <- simulate_renewal_forecast(   incidence = train$incidence,   serial_interval = serial_interval,   rt = rt_hat,   horizon = 7,   n_sims = 600,   seed = 77 )  epichains_cmp <- simulate_epichains_forecast(   initial_cases = max(1L, round(tail(train$incidence, 1))),   rt = rt_hat,   serial_interval = serial_interval,   horizon = 7,   n_sims = 600,   seed = 77 )  score_compare <- bind_rows(   score_forecast_draws(truth, renewal_cmp) |>     mutate(method = "renewal_poisson"),   score_forecast_draws(truth, epichains_cmp) |>     mutate(method = "epichains_branching") ) |>   group_by(method) |>   summarise(     mae = mean(mae),     rmse = sqrt(mean(sq_error)),     coverage = mean(covered),     wis = mean(wis),     crps = mean(crps),     .groups = "drop"   )  score_compare #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   method                mae  rmse coverage   wis  crps #>   <chr>               <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 epichains_branching  5     5.54    0.286  3.25  3.86 #> 2 renewal_poisson      2.14  2.59    1      1.10  1.40

title: Advanced count time-series uncertainty and forecast evaluation
text: package now supports complete uncertainty-aware workflow count forecasting: estimate trend parameters uncertainty draws, propagate uncertainty recursive renewal simulation, summarise predictive distributions, evaluate forecasts rolling-origin backtesting, report proper scoring rules (WIS, CRPS) alongside MAE/RMSE/coverage, compare chain-based simulation via epichains. components designed reproducible advanced teaching practical forecast model assessment count time-series settings.

what: Epidemiological and Statistical Workflows for Outbreak Analysis
title: Epidemiological and Statistical Workflows for Outbreak Analysis
text: omia R package epidemiological/statistical outbreak workflows, including: incidence data preparation time-varying Rt estimation (EpiEstim wrapper) renewal-equation expected incidence calculations Poisson process simulation Poisson growth model fitting doubling time uncertainty-propagating short-term forecasts growth models rolling-origin forecast evaluation (MAE/RMSE/coverage/WIS/CRPS) epichains-based branching-process simulation chain-aware uncertainty model comparison using AIC/BIC (optional LOOIC) epidemic Rt visualization outbreak consensus summaries

title: Epidemiological and Statistical Workflows for Outbreak Analysis
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_local(".")

title: Epidemiological and Statistical Workflows for Outbreak Analysis
code: library(omia)  data(cat_ba_week1)  inc <- prep_incidence_data(cat_ba_week1) growth <- fit_poisson_growth(inc) summary_tbl <- outbreak_consensus_summary(   incidence_data = inc,   rt_estimates = tibble::tibble(),   growth_fit = growth )  summary_tbl

title: Epidemiological and Statistical Workflows for Outbreak Analysis
text: functions use optional packages available: estimate_rt_epiestim() requires EpiEstim. compare_incidence_models(include_looic = TRUE) can use loo.

what: Evaluate forecasts with rolling-origin backtesting — evaluate_rolling_origin_forecast
title: Evaluate forecasts with rolling-origin backtesting — evaluate_rolling_origin_forecast
text: Repeatedly refits forecast_poisson_growth() expanding windows evaluates short-term --sample performance.

title: Evaluate forecasts with rolling-origin backtesting — evaluate_rolling_origin_forecast
code: evaluate_rolling_origin_forecast(   data,   date_col = "date",   count_col = "incidence",   initial_window,   horizon = 7L,   step = 1L,   level = 0.9,   n_draws = 1000L,   include_observation_uncertainty = TRUE,   mean_si = 4.7,   wis_levels = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95),   seed = NULL )

title: Evaluate forecasts with rolling-origin backtesting — evaluate_rolling_origin_forecast
text: data data frame date incidence columns. date_col Name date column. count_col Name incidence/count column. initial_window Initial training-window length. horizon Forecast horizon days. step Step size forecast origins. level Central prediction interval level coverage. n_draws Number forecast draws per origin. include_observation_uncertainty Whether include Poisson observation uncertainty forecast draws. mean_si Mean serial interval passed forecast_poisson_growth(). wis_levels Central interval levels used WIS. seed Optional random seed.

title: Evaluate forecasts with rolling-origin backtesting — evaluate_rolling_origin_forecast
text: list : scores: day-level scores origin/day, metrics_overall: aggregated overall metrics, metrics_by_horizon: metrics aggregated forecast horizon day, forecast_summaries: concatenated forecast summaries.

what: Fit a log-linear Poisson growth model — fit_poisson_growth
title: Fit a log-linear Poisson growth model — fit_poisson_growth
text: Fits Poisson regression incidence time reports growth rate, confidence interval, doubling time.

title: Fit a log-linear Poisson growth model — fit_poisson_growth
text: data data frame date incidence columns. date_col Name date column. count_col Name incidence/count column. n_draws Number uncertainty draws asymptotic Normal distribution growth coefficient. Set 0 skip. mean_si Mean serial interval used map growth-rate draws implied R_t draws via exp(growth_rate * mean_si). seed Optional random seed used n_draws > 0.

title: Fit a log-linear Poisson growth model — fit_poisson_growth
text: list model object, coefficient summary tibble, fitted values, (optionally) growth-rate uncertainty draws.

what: Forecast incidence from a Poisson growth model with uncertainty — forecast_poisson_growth
title: Forecast incidence from a Poisson growth model with uncertainty — forecast_poisson_growth
text: Fits Poisson log-linear growth model propagates coefficient uncertainty short-term forecasts, optional Poisson observation noise.

title: Forecast incidence from a Poisson growth model with uncertainty — forecast_poisson_growth
code: forecast_poisson_growth(   data,   date_col = "date",   count_col = "incidence",   horizon = 7L,   level = 0.9,   n_draws = 2000L,   include_observation_uncertainty = TRUE,   mean_si = 4.7,   seed = NULL )

title: Forecast incidence from a Poisson growth model with uncertainty — forecast_poisson_growth
text: data data frame date incidence columns. date_col Name date column. count_col Name incidence/count column. horizon Forecast horizon days. level Central prediction interval level (e.g. 0.9). n_draws Number forecast draws. include_observation_uncertainty TRUE, sample Poisson outcomes around expected incidence. FALSE, return expected-value draws . mean_si Mean serial interval passed fit_poisson_growth() implied R_t reporting. seed Optional random seed.

title: Forecast incidence from a Poisson growth model with uncertainty — forecast_poisson_growth
text: list components: forecast: day-level summary (date, day_ahead, mean_expected, median_forecast, lower, upper, level), draws: matrix predictive draws (n_draws x horizon), expected_draws: matrix expected-incidence draws, fit: output fit_poisson_growth().

what: Rolling-origin backtesting for count forecasts — rolling_origin_evaluate
title: Rolling-origin backtesting for count forecasts — rolling_origin_evaluate
text: Runs rolling-origin forecast evaluation repeatedly refitting expanding windows scoring --sample forecast horizons. default rt_method = "growth" maps growth-rate uncertainty fit_poisson_growth() R_t draws used simulate_renewal_forecast().

title: Rolling-origin backtesting for count forecasts — rolling_origin_evaluate
code: rolling_origin_evaluate(   data,   date_col = "date",   count_col = "incidence",   initial_window,   horizon = 7L,   step = 1L,   serial_interval,   rt_method = c("growth", "fixed"),   fixed_rt = 1,   n_sims = 500L,   mean_si = 4.7,   interval_level = 0.9,   wis_levels = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95),   seed = NULL )

title: Rolling-origin backtesting for count forecasts — rolling_origin_evaluate
text: data Data frame containing date incidence columns. date_col Name date column data. count_col Name incidence column data. initial_window Initial training-window length. horizon Forecast horizon origin. step Step size forecast origins. serial_interval Numeric serial interval probability vector. rt_method Method generating R_t forecasts ("growth" "fixed"). fixed_rt Scalar length-horizon vector fixed R_t values rt_method = "fixed". n_sims Number forecast simulations per origin. mean_si Mean serial interval used map growth-rate draws R_t draws rt_method = "growth". interval_level Central interval level coverage scoring. wis_levels Levels used WIS. seed Optional random seed.

title: Rolling-origin backtesting for count forecasts — rolling_origin_evaluate
text: list components: scores: day-level scores across origins, origin_summary: origin-level aggregated metrics, overall: global aggregated metrics, forecast_summary: predictive summary quantiles origin/day.

title: Score probabilistic count forecasts — score_forecast_draws
text: Computes point distributional forecast scores horizon day, including MAE, RMSE, interval coverage, WIS, CRPS.

title: Score probabilistic count forecasts — score_forecast_draws
code: score_forecast_draws(   observed,   forecast_draws,   point = c("median", "mean"),   interval_level = 0.9,   wis_levels = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95) )

title: Score probabilistic count forecasts — score_forecast_draws
text: observed Numeric vector observed counts forecast horizon. forecast_draws Forecast draws tibble columns sim, day, incidence, numeric matrix rows = simulations columns = horizon days. point Summary used point predictions ("median" "mean"). interval_level Central prediction-interval level coverage. wis_levels Central interval levels used weighted interval score.

title: Score probabilistic count forecasts — score_forecast_draws
text: tibble one row per day forecast score columns.

what: Simulate chain-based incidence forecasts with epichains — simulate_epichains_forecast
title: Simulate chain-based incidence forecasts with epichains — simulate_epichains_forecast
text: Uses epichains::simulate_chains() generate branching-process forecast trajectories specified number initial cases. provides chain-based alternative renewal-Poisson simulations.

title: Simulate chain-based incidence forecasts with epichains — simulate_epichains_forecast
code: simulate_epichains_forecast(   initial_cases,   rt,   serial_interval,   horizon = 14L,   n_sims = 500L,   stat_threshold = 10000,   pop = Inf,   seed = NULL )

title: Simulate chain-based incidence forecasts with epichains — simulate_epichains_forecast
text: initial_cases Number index cases forecast origin. rt Scalar reproduction number used Poisson offspring mean. serial_interval Numeric serial interval probability vector used sample discrete generation times. horizon Forecast horizon (days). n_sims Number chain simulations. stat_threshold Maximum simulated chain size used cap explosions. pop Susceptible population cap passed epichains. seed Optional random seed.

title: Simulate chain-based incidence forecasts with epichains — simulate_epichains_forecast
text: tibble columns sim, day, simulated incidence.

what: Simulate uncertainty-aware transmission chains with epichains — simulate_epichains_paths
title: Simulate uncertainty-aware transmission chains with epichains — simulate_epichains_paths
text: Simulates short-horizon incidence paths using epichains::simulate_chains(), allowing R_t uncertainty via scalar vector input.

title: Simulate uncertainty-aware transmission chains with epichains — simulate_epichains_paths
code: simulate_epichains_paths(   rt,   n_sims = 300L,   horizon = 7L,   offspring_dist = c("poisson", "nbinom"),   dispersion = 10,   seed = NULL )

title: Simulate uncertainty-aware transmission chains with epichains — simulate_epichains_paths
text: rt Reproduction number input. Scalar, vector draws sample across simulations. n_sims Number simulations. horizon Number forecast days (including day 0 raw output). offspring_dist Offspring distribution ("poisson" "nbinom"). dispersion Negative-binomial size parameter offspring_dist = "nbinom". seed Optional random seed.

title: Simulate uncertainty-aware transmission chains with epichains — simulate_epichains_paths
text: list : simulations: row-level simulated incidences sim day_ahead; summary: day-level mean/median/5th/95th quantiles.

what: Simulate renewal-based count forecasts with uncertainty propagation — simulate_renewal_forecast
title: Simulate renewal-based count forecasts with uncertainty propagation — simulate_renewal_forecast
text: Generates stochastic forecast trajectories renewal equation propagating uncertainty reproduction numbers (rt) process noise. Future incidence simulated recursively, uncertainty compounds full forecast horizon.

title: Simulate renewal-based count forecasts with uncertainty propagation — simulate_renewal_forecast
code: simulate_renewal_forecast(   incidence,   serial_interval,   rt = 1,   horizon = NULL,   n_sims = 1000L,   seed = NULL )

title: Simulate renewal-based count forecasts with uncertainty propagation — simulate_renewal_forecast
text: incidence Numeric vector historical incidence counts. serial_interval Numeric serial interval probability vector first element corresponds lag one time step. rt Reproduction-number input. Can : scalar (constant across simulations forecast days), vector length horizon (time-varying shared across simulations), matrix n_sims rows horizon columns (fully stochastic paths). horizon Number forecast days. NULL, inferred rt rt vector matrix. Defaults 7 rt scalar. n_sims Number simulation paths rt scalar vector. seed Optional random seed.

title: Simulate renewal-based count forecasts with uncertainty propagation — simulate_renewal_forecast
text: tibble one row per simulation/day containing sim, day, rt, infectiousness, expected_incidence, simulated incidence.

what: Summarise predictive uncertainty from forecast draws — summarise_forecast_uncertainty
title: Summarise predictive uncertainty from forecast draws — summarise_forecast_uncertainty
text: Summarise predictive uncertainty forecast draws

title: Summarise predictive uncertainty from forecast draws — summarise_forecast_uncertainty
code: summarise_forecast_uncertainty(   forecast_draws,   probs = c(0.05, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.95),   interval_level = 0.9 )

title: Summarise predictive uncertainty from forecast draws — summarise_forecast_uncertainty
text: forecast_draws Forecast draws tibble columns sim, day, incidence, numeric matrix rows = simulations, columns = forecast days. probs Quantiles report. interval_level Level central prediction interval.

title: Summarise predictive uncertainty from forecast draws — summarise_forecast_uncertainty
text: tibble one row per forecast day uncertainty summaries (mean, median, sd, interval bounds, requested quantiles).
